subject: HTTPS calls for sdc
commit_message:
HTTPS calls for sdc

Proper configuration for HTTPS in the sdc:
-configured the livenessProbe/readinessProbe ports
-changed mount path for dcea-be components
-updated dcea component images
-updated VID and Portal to talk with the SDC

Change-Id: Ibdece8f095aaa79e326cb9c4510e8227a7856b15
Issue-ID: SDC-2548
Signed-off-by: Krystian Kedron <k.kedron@partner.samsung.com>

Patch Set 4:

Where can I find an HTTPS-enabled SDC deployment (SNAPSHOTs are ok)?
I would like to see VID is communicating with.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> Where can I find an HTTPS-enabled SDC deployment (SNAPSHOTs are ok)?
> I would like to see VID is communicating with.

I updated the https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/95068/ commit to has the recent changes for VID, so I think you can test the VID communication with SDC basing on this.

Patch Set 4:

Isn't a little too late to introduce such change in EL Alto?

Patch Set 4:

> > Patch Set 4:
 > >
 > > Where can I find an HTTPS-enabled SDC deployment (SNAPSHOTs are
 > ok)?
 > > I would like to see VID is communicating with.
 > 
 > I updated the https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/95068/ commit to
 > has the recent changes for VID, so I think you can test the VID
 > communication with SDC basing on this.

Do we need to import SDC certificate to properly communicate with SDC over https?

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> > > Patch Set 4:
>  > >
>  > > Where can I find an HTTPS-enabled SDC deployment (SNAPSHOTs are
>  > ok)?
>  > > I would like to see VID is communicating with.
>  > 
>  > I updated the https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/95068/ commit to
>  > has the recent changes for VID, so I think you can test the VID
>  > communication with SDC basing on this.
> 
> Do we need to import SDC certificate to properly communicate with SDC over https?

SDC certificates were create using AAF framework, so for sure you will be need truststore (eq. catalog-be/sdc-backend/chef-repo/cookbooks/sdc-catalog-be/files/default/org.onap.sdc.trust.jks and truststore_pwd = "].][xgtze]hBhz*wy]}m#lf*" you can see this on this commit https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/sdc/+/95240) rest depends on your client which you using to communicate with SDC (some http client can disable validation of certificate option). DNS supported for the SDC certificate:

sdc
onap.org
sdc-be.*
sdc-dcae-be.*
sdc-dcae-dt.*
sdc-dcae-fe.*
sdc-dcae-tosca-lab.*
sdc-es.*
sdc-fe.*
sdc-kb.*
sdc-onboarding-be.*
sdc-wfd-be.*
sdc.api.fe.simpledemo.onap.org
sdc.api.simpledemo.onap.org
sdc.dcae.plugin.simpledemo.onap.org
sdc.workflow.plugin.simpledemo.onap.org
ssdc-wfd-fe.*
webseal.*

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> > Patch Set 4:
> > 
> > > > Patch Set 4:
> >  > >
> >  > > Where can I find an HTTPS-enabled SDC deployment (SNAPSHOTs are
> >  > ok)?
> >  > > I would like to see VID is communicating with.
> >  > 
> >  > I updated the https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/95068/ commit to
> >  > has the recent changes for VID, so I think you can test the VID
> >  > communication with SDC basing on this.
> > 
> > Do we need to import SDC certificate to properly communicate with SDC over https?
> 
> SDC certificates were create using AAF framework, so for sure you will be need truststore (eq. catalog-be/sdc-backend/chef-repo/cookbooks/sdc-catalog-be/files/default/org.onap.sdc.trust.jks and truststore_pwd = "].][xgtze]hBhz*wy]}m#lf*" you can see this on this commit https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/sdc/+/95240) rest depends on your client which you using to communicate with SDC (some http client can disable validation of certificate option). DNS supported for the SDC certificate:
> 
> sdc
> onap.org
> sdc-be.*
> sdc-dcae-be.*
> sdc-dcae-dt.*
> sdc-dcae-fe.*
> sdc-dcae-tosca-lab.*
> sdc-es.*
> sdc-fe.*
> sdc-kb.*
> sdc-onboarding-be.*
> sdc-wfd-be.*
> sdc.api.fe.simpledemo.onap.org
> sdc.api.simpledemo.onap.org
> sdc.dcae.plugin.simpledemo.onap.org
> sdc.workflow.plugin.simpledemo.onap.org
> ssdc-wfd-fe.*
> webseal.*

We will see if VID works out of the box with no change.
If not, we will evaluate any needed change. (It might be that some changes will be available out of El-Altos's timeframe.)
